passage_title: The life and work of Marie Curie

sentences.sentence: Marie Curie is probably the most famous woman scientist who has ever lived.
sentences.translation: 玛丽·居里或许是有史以来最著名的女科学家。

structure.text: probably the most famous woman scientist

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 the most famous woman scientist。
has ever lived 使用现在完成时，与最高级连用，表达“有史以来”。

expressions.text: the most famous ... who has ever lived

sentences.sentence: Born Maria Sklodowska in Poland in 1867, she is famous for her work on radioactivity, and was twice a winner of the Nobel Prize.
sentences.translation: 她于1867年出生在波兰，原名玛丽亚·斯克沃多夫斯卡，因放射性研究而闻名，并曾两度获得诺贝尔奖。

structure.text: Born Maria Sklodowska in Poland in 1867
is famous for her work on radioactivity
and was twice a winner of the Nobel Prize

difficult_points: Born... 是过去分词状语，逻辑主语是 she，补充其原名、出生地和年份。
is famous 和 was a winner 是时态不同的并列谓语：前者说现今声誉，后者说过去获奖事实。

expressions.text: a winner of the Nobel Prize

sentences.sentence: With her husband, Pierre Curie, and Henri Becquerel, she was awarded the 1903 Nobel Prize for Physics, and was then sole winner of the 1911 Nobel Prize for Chemistry.
sentences.translation: 她与丈夫皮埃尔·居里及亨利·贝克勒尔共同获得1903年诺贝尔物理学奖，随后又独自获得1911年诺贝尔化学奖。

structure.text: With her husband, Pierre Curie, and Henri Becquerel
was awarded the 1903 Nobel Prize for Physics
and was then sole winner of the 1911 Nobel Prize for Chemistry

difficult_points: Pierre Curie 是 her husband 的同位语；With 短语说明她与谁共同获奖。
was awarded 是被动语态，而 sole winner 强调1911年的奖项由她独自获得。

sentences.sentence: She was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize.
sentences.translation: 她是第一位获得诺贝尔奖的女性。
difficult_points: to win... 后置修饰 the first woman，说明“第一位”完成的事情。
the first + 名词 + to do 是表达“第一个做某事的人或事物”的常见结构。

sentences.sentence: From childhood, Marie was remarkable for her prodigious memory, and at the age of 16 won a gold medal on completion of her secondary education.
sentences.translation: 玛丽自幼便以惊人的记忆力出众，并在16岁完成中等教育时获得一枚金牌。

structure.text: was remarkable for her prodigious memory
and at the age of 16 won a gold medal
on completion of her secondary education

difficult_points: was remarkable 和 won 是共用主语 Marie 的并列谓语。
on completion of... 表示“一完成……便”，说明她获金牌的时间节点。

sentences.sentence: Because her father lost his savings through bad investment, she then had to take work as a teacher.
sentences.translation: 由于父亲投资失误损失了积蓄，她后来不得不做教师工作。

structure.text: Because her father lost his savings through bad investment

difficult_points: Because 引导原因状语从句，解释她为何必须工作。
had to 表示客观处境迫使她如此；as a teacher 说明 work 的身份性质。

sentences.sentence: From her earnings she was able to finance her sister Bronia's medical studies in Paris, on the understanding that Bronia would, in turn, later help her to get an education.
sentences.translation: 她用自己的收入资助姐姐布罗尼娅在巴黎学医，双方约定布罗尼娅日后也会反过来帮助她接受教育。

structure.text: her sister Bronia's medical studies in Paris
on the understanding that Bronia would, in turn, later help her to get an education
that Bronia would, in turn, later help her to get an education

difficult_points: on the understanding that... 表示采取行动所依据的约定或条件。
in turn 是插入语，表示布罗尼娅日后会反过来帮助玛丽；her 指 Marie。

sentences.sentence: In 1891 this promise was fulfilled and Marie went to Paris and began to study at the Sorbonne (the University of Paris).
sentences.translation: 1891年，这一承诺得以兑现，玛丽前往巴黎，开始在索邦大学（巴黎大学）学习。

structure.text: and began to study at the Sorbonne (the University of Paris)

difficult_points: and 连接承诺兑现与玛丽赴巴黎学习的后续事件；第二个 and 连接 went 和 began。
the University of Paris 是 the Sorbonne 的括号同位说明。

sentences.sentence: She often worked far into the night and lived on little more than bread and butter and tea.
sentences.translation: 她经常工作到深夜，生活中几乎只有面包、黄油和茶果腹。

structure.text: often worked far into the night
and lived on little more than bread and butter and tea

difficult_points: worked far into the night 表示工作一直持续到很晚，而非在夜里走得很远。
live on 表示依靠某种食物维生；little more than 强调食物极其有限。

sentences.sentence: She came first in the examination in the physical sciences in 1893, and in 1894 was placed second in the examination in mathematical sciences.
sentences.translation: 1893年，她在物理科学考试中名列第一；1894年，又在数学科学考试中获得第二名。

structure.text: came first in the examination in the physical sciences in 1893
and in 1894 was placed second in the examination in mathematical sciences

difficult_points: come first 表示考试排名第一；was placed second 是被动表达，表示被评为第二名。
and 后省略了主语 she，两个并列谓语分别对应1893和1894年。

sentences.sentence: It was not until the spring of that year that she was introduced to Pierre Curie.
sentences.translation: 直到那年春天，她才经人介绍认识皮埃尔·居里。

structure.text: It was not until the spring of that year that she was introduced to Pierre Curie
not until the spring of that year
she was introduced to Pierre Curie

difficult_points: It was not until... that... 是强调句，表示“直到……才……”。
was introduced to 是被动语态，表示有人将她介绍给皮埃尔·居里；that year 指1894年。

sentences.sentence: Their marriage in 1895 marked the start of a partnership that was soon to achieve results of world significance.
sentences.translation: 他们1895年的婚姻标志着一段合作关系的开始，而这段合作很快便取得了具有世界意义的成果。

structure.text: that was soon to achieve results of world significance

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 a partnership，而不是 marriage。
was to achieve 表示后来注定或即将取得某种结果；of world significance 表示意义重大且影响全球。

sentences.sentence: Following Henri Becquerel's discovery in 1896 of a new phenomenon, which Marie later called 'radioactivity', Marie Curie decided to find out if the radioactivity discovered in uranium was to be found in other elements.
sentences.translation: 继亨利·贝克勒尔于1896年发现一种新现象之后——玛丽后来将其称为“放射性”——玛丽·居里决定查明在铀中发现的放射性是否也存在于其他元素中。

structure.text: Following Henri Becquerel's discovery in 1896 of a new phenomenon
if the radioactivity discovered in uranium was to be found in other elements

difficult_points: Following 在句首作介词，表示“在……之后”；which 回指 a new phenomenon。
if 引导 find out 的宾语从句，表示“是否”。
discovered in uranium 修饰 radioactivity；was to be found 表示预期或可能在其他元素中发现。

sentences.sentence: She discovered that this was true for thorium.
sentences.translation: 她发现，钍也具有这种性质。

structure.text: that this was true for thorium

difficult_points: this 回指“铀中的放射性也能在其他元素中发现”这一判断。
be true for thorium 表示该情况同样适用于钍。

sentences.sentence: Turning her attention to minerals, she found her interest drawn to pitchblende, a mineral whose radioactivity, superior to that of pure uranium, could be explained only by the presence in the ore of small quantities of an unknown substance of very high activity.
sentences.translation: 把注意力转向矿物后，她对沥青铀矿产生了兴趣；这种矿物的放射性强于纯铀，而这只能用矿石中存在少量高活性未知物质来解释。

structure.text: whose radioactivity, superior to that of pure uranium, could be explained only by the presence in the ore of small quantities of an unknown substance of very high activity
superior to that of pure uranium

difficult_points: Turning... 是现在分词状语，逻辑主语为 she；find + 宾语 + drawn 构成复合宾语。
a mineral 是 pitchblende 的同位语，whose 引导定语从句并在从句中限定 radioactivity。
superior to that of... 中 that 代替 radioactivity；could be explained only by 表示唯一可能的解释。

sentences.sentence: Pierre Curie joined her in the work that she had undertaken to resolve this problem, and that led to the discovery of the new elements, polonium and radium.
sentences.translation: 皮埃尔·居里加入了她为解决这一问题而开展的研究，这项工作最终促成了新元素钋和镭的发现。

structure.text: that she had undertaken to resolve this problem
and that led to the discovery of the new elements, polonium and radium

difficult_points: 两个 that 从句都修饰 the work：前者说明她已开展的工作，后者说明工作带来的结果。
had undertaken 是过去完成时，表示玛丽开展研究先于皮埃尔加入。
polonium and radium 是 the new elements 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: While Pierre Curie devoted himself chiefly to the physical study of the new radiations, Marie Curie struggled to obtain pure radium in the metallic state.
sentences.translation: 皮埃尔·居里主要致力于研究新辐射的物理性质，而玛丽·居里则努力获取金属状态的纯镭。

structure.text: While Pierre Curie devoted himself chiefly to the physical study of the new radiations
pure radium in the metallic state

difficult_points: While 在此主要表示对比两人的研究分工，而非单纯表示同时发生。
devote oneself to 中 to 是介词；struggle to obtain 表示为达到困难目标而努力。

sentences.sentence: This was achieved with the help of the chemist André
sentences.translation: 这一目标在化学家安德烈·路易·德比耶纳的帮助下得以实现。

structure.text: with the help of the chemist André

difficult_points: This 回指上一句“获得金属状态的纯镭”这一目标。
源文在段落边界处把姓名 André-Louis Debierne 拆开，本段末的 André 与下一段开头 -Louis Debierne 连读。

expressions.text: be achieved with the help of

sentences.sentence: -Louis Debierne, one of Pierre Curie's pupils.
sentences.translation: （接上段）路易·德比耶纳是皮埃尔·居里的学生之一。
difficult_points: 源文在段落边界处错误拆分了 André-Louis Debierne，本片段需与上一段末尾 André 合并理解。
one of Pierre Curie's pupils 是 André-Louis Debierne 的同位语，补充其身份。

sentences.sentence: Based on the results of this research, Marie Curie received her Doctorate of Science, and in 1903 Marie and Pierre shared with Becquerel the Nobel Prize for Physics for the discovery of radioactivity.
sentences.translation: 凭借这项研究的成果，玛丽·居里获得了理学博士学位；1903年，她与皮埃尔又因发现放射性而同贝克勒尔共同获得诺贝尔物理学奖。

structure.text: Based on the results of this research
and in 1903 Marie and Pierre shared with Becquerel the Nobel Prize for Physics for the discovery of radioactivity

difficult_points: Based on... 表示获得学位所依据的研究成果，逻辑上修饰主句事件。
and 后是主语 Marie and Pierre 的新分句；shared with Becquerel 表示三人共同获奖。
for the discovery of radioactivity 说明授奖原因，而非奖项用途。

sentences.sentence: The births of Marie's two daughters, Irène and Eve, in 1897 and 1904 failed to interrupt her scientific work.
sentences.translation: 玛丽的两个女儿伊雷娜和伊芙分别于1897年和1904年出生，但这并未中断她的科学工作。

structure.text: The births of Marie's two daughters, Irène and Eve, in 1897 and 1904

difficult_points: Irène and Eve 是 Marie's two daughters 的同位语，两个年份分别对应两次出生。
fail to interrupt 表示本可能造成中断的事件实际上未能影响她的科研。

sentences.sentence: She was appointed lecturer in physics at the École Normale Supérieure for girls in Sèvres, France (1900), and introduced a method of teaching based on experimental demonstrations.
sentences.translation: 1900年，她被任命为法国塞夫尔女子高等师范学校的物理学讲师，并引入了一种以实验演示为基础的教学方法。

structure.text: was appointed lecturer in physics at the École Normale Supérieure for girls in Sèvres, France (1900)

difficult_points: was appointed 后直接接职位 lecturer，表示被任命担任该职。
introduced 与 was appointed 并列，但采用主动语态；based on... 修饰教学方法。

sentences.sentence: In December 1904 she was appointed chief assistant in the laboratory directed by Pierre Curie.
sentences.translation: 1904年12月，她被任命为皮埃尔·居里主持的实验室的首席助理。
difficult_points: chief assistant 是 was appointed 后的主语补足语，说明她获任的职位。
directed by Pierre Curie 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 laboratory。

sentences.sentence: The sudden death of her husband in 1906 was a bitter blow to Marie Curie, but was also a turning point in her career: henceforth she was to devote all her energy to completing alone the scientific work that they had undertaken.
sentences.translation: 丈夫于1906年突然去世，对玛丽·居里是沉重打击，却也成为她事业的转折点：从此以后，她将把全部精力投入到独自完成两人已经开展的科研工作中。

structure.text: The sudden death of her husband in 1906
was a bitter blow to Marie Curie
but was also a turning point in her career
henceforth she was to devote all her energy to completing alone the scientific work that they had undertaken

difficult_points: but 连接两个系表结构，第二个分句省略共同主语 The sudden death...。
冒号后的分句解释这个转折点如何改变她的事业；was to devote 表示此后注定或决定要做的事。
devote... to 中 to 是介词，后接 completing；that 修饰 the scientific work，they 指夫妇二人。

sentences.sentence: On May 13, 1906, she was appointed to the professorship that had been left vacant on her husband's death, becoming the first woman to teach at the Sorbonne.
sentences.translation: 1906年5月13日，她被任命接替因丈夫去世而空缺的教授职位，成为第一位在索邦大学任教的女性。

structure.text: that had been left vacant on her husband's death
becoming the first woman to teach at the Sorbonne

difficult_points: that 修饰 professorship；had been left vacant 是过去完成时被动，说明职位在她获任前已经空缺。
on her husband's death 中 on 表示“在……发生之时或之后”。
becoming... 是结果状语，to teach... 修饰 the first woman。

sentences.sentence: In 1911 she was awarded the Nobel Prize for Chemistry for the isolation of a pure form of radium.
sentences.translation: 1911年，她因成功分离出纯镭而获得诺贝尔化学奖。

structure.text: for the isolation of a pure form of radium

difficult_points: was awarded 是双宾语结构 award sb sth 的被动形式。
第一个 for 属于奖项名称 Nobel Prize for Chemistry，第二个 for 说明获奖原因。

sentences.sentence: During World War I, Marie Curie, with the help of her daughter Irène, devoted herself to the development of the use of X-radiography, including the mobile units which came to be known as 'little Curies', used for the treatment of wounded soldiers.
sentences.translation: 第一次世界大战期间，玛丽·居里在女儿伊雷娜的帮助下，致力于发展X射线摄影技术的应用，其中包括后来被称为“小居里”的移动设备，用于救治伤员。

structure.text: with the help of her daughter Irène
the development of the use of X-radiography
which came to be known as 'little Curies'
used for the treatment of wounded soldiers

difficult_points: with the help of... 是插入状语；devote oneself to 中 to 是介词，后接名词 development。
which 修饰 mobile units，came to be known as 表示后来逐渐获得这一称呼。
used for... 是过去分词短语，也修饰 mobile units，说明用途。

expressions.text: be used for the treatment of

sentences.sentence: In 1918 the Radium Institute, whose staff Irène had joined, began to operate in earnest, and became a center for nuclear physics and chemistry.
sentences.translation: 1918年，伊雷娜已经加入其团队的镭研究所开始全面运作，并成为核物理与核化学研究中心。

structure.text: and became a center for nuclear physics and chemistry

difficult_points: whose staff 引导非限制性定语从句，表示伊雷娜已加入该研究所的工作人员队伍。
operate in earnest 表示研究所开始认真而全面地运转，而非刚进行试验性活动。

sentences.sentence: Marie Curie, now at the highest point of her fame and, from 1922, a member of the Academy of Medicine, researched the chemistry of radioactive substances and their medical applications.
sentences.translation: 此时已处于声望巅峰、并从1922年起成为医学科学院院士的玛丽·居里，研究了放射性物质的化学性质及其医学应用。

structure.text: now at the highest point of her fame
from 1922, a member of the Academy of Medicine
the chemistry of radioactive substances and their medical applications

difficult_points: 主语 Marie Curie 与谓语 researched 之间插入两项身份和状态说明。
their 回指 radioactive substances，medical applications 指这些物质在医学中的用途。

expressions.text: at the highest point of one's fame

sentences.sentence: In 1921, accompanied by her two daughters, Marie Curie made a triumphant journey to the United States to raise funds for research on radium.
sentences.translation: 1921年，玛丽·居里在两个女儿陪同下成功访问美国，为镭研究筹集资金。

structure.text: made a triumphant journey to the United States
to raise funds for research on radium

difficult_points: accompanied by... 是过去分词状语，逻辑主语是 Marie Curie。
to raise funds... 是目的状语，说明此次美国之行的目的。

sentences.sentence: Women there presented her with a gram of radium for her campaign.
sentences.translation: 当地妇女为支持她的活动，赠予她一克镭。
difficult_points: present sb with sth 是固定结构，表示正式地赠予某人某物。
there 指美国；for her campaign 说明这份镭用于支持她的筹款和研究活动。

sentences.sentence: Marie also gave lectures in Belgium, Brazil, Spain and Czechoslovakia and, in addition, had the satisfaction of seeing the development of the Curie Foundation in Paris, and the inauguration in 1932 in Warsaw of the Radium Institute, where her sister Bronia became director.
sentences.translation: 玛丽还在比利时、巴西、西班牙和捷克斯洛伐克讲学；此外，她欣慰地看到巴黎居里基金会不断发展，并见证了镭研究所于1932年在华沙成立，她的姐姐布罗尼娅在那里担任所长。

structure.text: also gave lectures in Belgium, Brazil, Spain and Czechoslovakia
and, in addition, had the satisfaction of seeing
the development of the Curie Foundation in Paris, and the inauguration in 1932 in Warsaw of the Radium Institute
where her sister Bronia became director

difficult_points: gave 和 had 是共用主语 Marie 的并列谓语；in addition 是插入的连接语。
seeing 后接 the development 和 the inauguration 两个并列名词宾语。
where 修饰 the Radium Institute，说明她姐姐在那里担任所长。

sentences.sentence: One of Marie Curie's outstanding achievements was to have understood the need to accumulate intense radioactive sources, not only to treat illness but also to maintain an abundant supply for research.
sentences.translation: 玛丽·居里的一项杰出成就，是认识到必须积累强放射源，不仅用于治疗疾病，也要为研究保持充足的供应。

structure.text: One of Marie Curie's outstanding achievements
to have understood the need to accumulate intense radioactive sources
not only to treat illness but also to maintain an abundant supply for research

difficult_points: to have understood 是完成式不定式作表语，强调她早已认识到这一需要。
the need to accumulate 中不定式后置说明 need 的内容。
not only... but also... 连接两个积累放射源的目的。

sentences.sentence: The existence in Paris at the Radium Institute of a stock of 1.5 grams of radium made a decisive contribution to the success of the experiments undertaken in the years around 1930.
sentences.translation: 巴黎镭研究所储存的1.5克镭，对1930年前后开展的实验取得成功起到了决定性作用。

structure.text: The existence in Paris at the Radium Institute of a stock of 1.5 grams of radium
a decisive contribution to the success of the experiments

difficult_points: 主语中心词是 existence，in Paris、at the Radium Institute 和 of a stock... 逐层说明存在何物及地点。
undertaken... 是过去分词短语，修饰 experiments。

sentences.sentence: This work prepared the way for the discovery of the neutron by Sir James Chadwick and, above all, for the discovery in 1934 by Irène and Frédéric Joliot-Curie of artificial radioactivity.
sentences.translation: 这项工作为詹姆斯·查德威克爵士发现中子铺平了道路，更重要的是，也为伊雷娜和弗雷德里克·约里奥-居里于1934年发现人工放射性奠定了基础。

structure.text: for the discovery of the neutron by Sir James Chadwick
and, above all, for the discovery in 1934 by Irène and Frédéric Joliot-Curie of artificial radioactivity

difficult_points: prepare the way for 表示为后来成果创造条件，并非直接完成这些发现。
两个 for 短语并列；above all 插入强调人工放射性的发现尤其重要。
第二个 discovery 的时间、发现者和对象被多个短语分隔，需要还原为 the discovery of artificial radioactivity。

sentences.sentence: A few months after this discovery, Marie Curie died as a result of leukaemia caused by exposure to radiation.
sentences.translation: 这一发现几个月后，玛丽·居里因长期接触辐射所致的白血病去世。

structure.text: A few months after this discovery

difficult_points: this discovery 指上一句1934年人工放射性的发现。
caused by... 修饰 leukaemia，说明白血病由辐射暴露造成，而不是修饰 Marie Curie。

sentences.sentence: She had often carried test tubes containing radioactive isotopes in her pocket, remarking on the pretty blue-green light they gave off.
sentences.translation: 她过去常把装有放射性同位素的试管放在口袋里，还会赞叹它们发出的漂亮蓝绿色光芒。

structure.text: remarking on the pretty blue-green light they gave off

difficult_points: had carried 是过去完成时，回顾她去世前长期存在的习惯；containing... 修饰 test tubes。
remarking... 是伴随状语；the light 后省略关系词 that，they 指 radioactive isotopes 或装有它们的试管。
give off 是固定短语，表示发出光、热或气体。

sentences.sentence: Her contribution to physics had been immense, not only in her own work, the importance of which had been demonstrated by her two Nobel Prizes, but because of her influence on subsequent generations of nuclear physicists and chemists.
sentences.translation: 她对物理学的贡献极其巨大，不仅体现在她自己的研究上——两次诺贝尔奖已经证明了这些研究的重要性——也体现在她对后来几代核物理学家和化学家的影响上。

structure.text: not only in her own work
the importance of which had been demonstrated by her two Nobel Prizes
but because of her influence on subsequent generations of nuclear physicists and chemists

difficult_points: not only... but... 连接她贡献巨大的两个方面：个人研究和对后人的影响。
the importance of which 中 which 指 her own work，介词 of 前置。
had been demonstrated 是过去完成时被动，说明两次诺贝尔奖已经证明其研究价值。
